mission.name: Lost in the Maze
mission.description: A configuration file is buried somewhere inside a nested directory tree inside the sandbox.
mission.objective: Find the file named 'secret.conf' and print its contents

mission.name: Build the Scaffold
mission.description: A web app expects a specific directory layout to exist before it starts. Without it, it crashes.
mission.objective: Create the directory tree: logs/app, logs/nginx, data/uploads, data/cache inside the sandbox

mission.name: Spring Cleaning
mission.description: The disk is filling up with leftover .tmp files scattered across the sandbox.
mission.objective: Delete all files ending in .tmp from the sandbox (leave everything else intact)

mission.name: The Great Migration
mission.description: A deployment script dropped all config files into the wrong directory. They need to be moved to their correct locations.
mission.objective: Move all .conf files from staging/ into production/ and rename app.conf.bak to app.conf

mission.name: Needle in a Haystack
mission.description: A developer lost track of all Python files they created across several project directories.
mission.objective: Find every file ending in .py under the sandbox and write their paths to found.txt in the sandbox root

mission.name: The Password Leak
mission.description: Someone hardcoded a password in one of the config files. Security needs you to find which file contains the string 'password='.
mission.objective: Find the file containing 'password=' and write its path to leak.txt in the sandbox root

mission.name: The Shortcut
mission.description: An app always reads its config from /etc/myapp/active.conf but configs are versioned. You need to point active.conf at the correct version.
mission.objective: Create a symlink at $SANDBOX/etc/myapp/active.conf pointing to $SANDBOX/etc/myapp/v2/config.conf

mission.name: Pack and Unpack
mission.description: A backup archive arrived but the files need to be extracted. Then you must archive the results directory.
mission.objective: Extract backup.tar.gz into extracted/, then create archive.tar.gz containing all .log files from logs/

mission.name: Disk Hog
mission.description: The server disk is nearly full. You need to identify the largest files and directories.
mission.objective: Find the single largest file in the sandbox and write its path to biggest.txt in the sandbox root

mission.name: Secrets in Plain Sight
mission.description: Hidden dotfiles are used to configure many Linux tools. A token file is hiding in the sandbox — but ls alone won't show it.
mission.objective: Find the hidden file (starting with .) containing 'AUTH_TOKEN', read its value, and copy it to token.txt in the sandbox root

mission.name: Log Triage
mission.description: A service crashed and left a huge log file. You need to see the first and last entries quickly.
mission.objective: Write the first 5 lines of app.log to first5.txt and the last 5 lines to last5.txt

mission.name: Error Extraction
mission.description: Ops needs a report of all ERROR and FATAL lines from the log for the post-mortem.
mission.objective: Extract all lines containing ERROR or FATAL from app.log and save to errors.txt

mission.name: By the Numbers
mission.description: The product team wants a quick report on the access log: total requests, unique IPs, and total bytes served.
mission.objective: Write a report.txt with 3 lines: total request count, unique IP count, and total bytes (sum of field 3)

mission.name: Top Talkers
mission.description: Security wants to know which IPs made the most requests — a classic log analysis task.
mission.objective: Produce a ranked list of IPs by request count (highest first) and save to top_ips.txt

mission.name: Find and Replace
mission.description: The staging config file still has old hostname references that need to be updated for production.
mission.objective: Replace all occurrences of 'staging.internal' with 'prod.internal' in config.conf and save to config.prod.conf

mission.name: Column Harvest
mission.description: A CSV export contains many columns but the billing system only needs usernames and email addresses.
mission.objective: Extract only the username (field 1) and email (field 3) from users.csv, save to extract.csv (comma-separated)

mission.name: Pipeline Master
mission.description: A multi-step data transformation: filter, transform, sort, and count — all in one pipeline.
mission.objective: From access.log, extract only POST requests, count how many came from each IP, save ranked result to post_ips.txt

mission.name: Spot the Difference
mission.description: Two versions of a config file exist. You need to see what changed between them and document the differences.
mission.objective: Compare v1.conf and v2.conf using diff and save the output to changes.diff

mission.name: Script Won't Run
mission.description: A deployment script was uploaded but it refuses to run — permission denied.
mission.objective: Make deploy.sh executable by the owner, then run it to generate output.txt

mission.name: Octal Surgery
mission.description: Three files need exact permission settings for a web server to function securely.
mission.objective: Set permissions: index.html→644, app.py→755, secrets.conf→600

mission.name: Wrong Owner
mission.description: Files were created as root but the app runs as 'nobody'. The app can't read its own config.
mission.objective: Change ownership of all files in config/ so the current user owns them (use your actual username)

mission.name: Shared Space
mission.description: A shared uploads directory lets any user write files — but users are deleting each other's work!
mission.objective: Set the sticky bit on the shared/ directory so only file owners can delete their own files

mission.name: The Audit Trail
mission.description: A shell script logs who runs it. You must identify which files in the sandbox have SUID or SGID bits set — a common security audit task.
mission.objective: Find all files with SUID or SGID bits set and save their paths to suid_files.txt

mission.name: Default Lockdown
mission.description: New files created by an app have world-writable permissions. The default umask is too permissive.
mission.objective: Understand what umask value produces 640 for files and 750 for dirs. Write the answer to umask_answer.txt

mission.name: Web Server 403
mission.description: Nginx returns 403 Forbidden on every request. The web root directory and files have wrong permissions.
mission.objective: Fix the web root: directories need 755, HTML files need 644, and the webroot itself must be readable

mission.name: World Writable Files
mission.description: Security scan flagged world-writable files in the app directory. These are a security risk — any user can modify them.
mission.objective: Find all world-writable files and remove write permission from 'others' for each one

mission.name: Process Detective
mission.description: Something is consuming CPU. You need to identify the top CPU-consuming processes and document them.
mission.objective: List the top 5 CPU-consuming processes and save the output to top_procs.txt

mission.name: Runaway Process
mission.description: A background script is running in an infinite loop and wasting resources. It needs to be stopped.
mission.objective: Find the 'sleep_loop.sh' process and kill it

mission.name: The Background Worker
mission.description: A data processing script takes a while to finish. You need to start it without blocking your terminal, inspect the job list, and let it complete.
mission.objective: Run process.sh in the background and wait for it to finish so result.txt contains the finished output

mission.name: Play Nice
mission.description: A CPU-heavy batch task is competing with other services. Launch it with a lower priority so it stays out of the way.
mission.objective: Run heavy.sh with niceness 15, save its PID to pid.txt, and confirm the process has the adjusted niceness

mission.name: Signal Relay
mission.description: A long-running listener reloads its configuration when it receives SIGHUP. Trigger that behavior without killing the service.
mission.objective: Send SIGHUP to listener.sh so it writes 'reloaded' to reload.log

mission.name: Zombie Apocalypse
mission.description: A buggy parent process left behind zombie children. Identify them, document what you found, and stop the parent so the zombies can be reaped.
mission.objective: Find the zombie processes, save their PIDs and names to zombies.txt, then kill the parent process

mission.name: Speak Variable
mission.description: Your onboarding script should greet whoever runs it, but the dynamic name support was never written.
mission.objective: Write greet.sh so running `bash greet.sh World` prints `Hello, World!`

mission.name: The Gatekeeper
mission.description: An automation script should tell you whether a target file exists, but the conditional logic is missing.
mission.objective: Write check_file.sh so it prints `EXISTS` when the path exists and `MISSING` when it does not

mission.name: Bulk Renamer
mission.description: A batch of text files needs to be archived with a new extension, and doing them one by one is not an option.
mission.objective: Write rename.sh so it renames every `.txt` file in the target directory to `.bak`

mission.name: DRY Deploy
mission.description: A repetitive deploy script works, but it is painful to maintain. Refactor it so one function handles each service.
mission.objective: Rewrite deploy.sh to use a function that deploys `api`, `web`, and `worker`

mission.name: Config Parser
mission.description: Your app stores environment variables in a `key=value` file. You need a loader script that turns those lines into exported shell variables.
mission.objective: Write load_env.sh so sourcing it with app.env exports APP_NAME, APP_ENV, and APP_PORT

mission.name: Fail Loudly
mission.description: A fragile script crashes in confusing ways. Harden it so failures stop early and missing input gets a clear error message.
mission.objective: Fix safe_copy.sh so it uses strict mode and prints `SOURCE_FILE is required` when no source path is passed

mission.name: Parallel Launch
mission.description: Three independent tasks take too long one after another. Launch them in parallel and wait for all of them to finish.
mission.objective: Write run_all.sh so it runs task-a.sh, task-b.sh, and task-c.sh in parallel and waits until all three finish

mission.name: Template Engine
mission.description: A config file should be generated from environment values, but the template logic was never written.
mission.objective: Write generate.sh so it reads vars.env and writes app.conf using a heredoc with the configured values

mission.name: What's My IP?
mission.description: You were given a saved `ip addr` capture and need to summarize which interfaces have which addresses.
mission.objective: Extract each interface and IPv4 address from ip_addr.txt and save them to interfaces.txt

mission.name: Can You Hear Me?
mission.description: Your sandbox includes a mock ping helper that simulates which hosts respond. Use it to document reachability.
mission.objective: Check every host in hosts.txt and save only the reachable hostnames to reachable.txt

mission.name: What's Listening?
mission.description: You have a saved socket listing from a host and need to document which TCP ports are listening.
mission.objective: Extract the listening TCP port numbers from ss-output.txt and save them to listening.txt

mission.name: API Call
mission.description: The API response is stored locally, but you still need to fetch it with curl and extract one field from the JSON.
mission.objective: Use curl on response.json and save the value of `service.status` to status.txt

mission.name: Name Resolution
mission.description: DNS lookups are mocked in this sandbox. Resolve the provided hostnames and record the answers.
mission.objective: Resolve each hostname in hosts.txt with mock_dig.sh and save `host ip` pairs to resolved.txt

mission.name: Connection Map
mission.description: A saved TCP snapshot shows active sessions. Count how many unique remote IPs have ESTABLISHED connections.
mission.objective: Count the unique remote IPs from ESTAB rows in connections.txt and save the number to unique_ips.txt

mission.name: Open Sesame
mission.description: A firewall ruleset was exported from a host. Read it and figure out which application port is being denied.
mission.objective: Find the blocked destination port in firewall.rules and write it to blocked_port.txt

mission.name: Packet Sniff
mission.description: A packet capture summary was exported from tcpdump. Identify which protocol the captured traffic belongs to.
mission.objective: Inspect capture.txt and write the protocol name to protocol.txt

mission.name: Service Down
mission.description: A dummy service is packaged in the sandbox along with a mock `systemctl` helper. Use it to enable and start the service.
mission.objective: Use the provided mock systemctl to enable and start myapp.service

mission.name: Log Hunt
mission.description: A mock `journalctl` command in the sandbox can show recent logs. Find the error entries from the last hour and save them.
mission.objective: Use the provided journalctl helper and save the recent `error` entries to errors.txt

mission.name: Scheduled Sweep
mission.description: A daily cleanup task needs to run automatically. Write the cron entry in the sandbox's crontab file.
mission.objective: Write a crontab entry in crontab.txt that runs cleanup.sh every day at 3am

mission.name: New Hire
mission.description: A mock user database in the sandbox needs a new developer account. Use the provided helpers to add the user and put them in the docker group.
mission.objective: Use the sandbox helpers to create `devuser` and add them to the `docker` group

mission.name: Controlled Power
mission.description: The ops team wants one narrowly scoped sudo permission. Write the rule into the sandbox's sudoers include file.
mission.objective: Write a rule in sudoers.d/devuser that allows devuser to run /usr/bin/systemctl without a password

mission.name: Log Discipline
mission.description: An application is filling disk with old logs. Write a logrotate config so the logs are rotated daily and compressed.
mission.objective: Create myapp.logrotate for `/var/log/myapp/*.log` with `daily`, `rotate 7`, and `compress`

mission.name: Env Surgery
mission.description: A startup script expects environment variables from a `.env` file, but the file is incomplete.
mission.objective: Fill in `.env` so sourcing it sets APP_MODE=production and APP_PORT=8080, then run app.sh successfully

mission.objective: Extract all IPv4 addresses from app.log and save them to ips.txt

mission.name: Config Injector
